title: minuartia juniperina var. glandulosa
intro: Minuartia juniperina var. glandulosa, also known as a glandular juniper-sage or juniper-sage, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Minuartia juniperina var. glandulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from digestive issues such as diarrhea and nausea. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
